# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: ci-cd-fast

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ci-cd-fast-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write

env:
  NODE_ENV: production

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      image: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.image }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - id: meta
        run: |
          IMAGE="ghcr.io/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY,,}:${GITHUB_SHA}"
          echo "image=$IMAGE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          file: ./Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.image }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    environment:
      name: production
    steps:
      - name: Deploy via SSH (remote pulls and restarts)
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.2.0
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}
          port: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PORT || 22 }}
          script: |
            set -euo pipefail
            IMAGE="${{ needs.build.outputs.image }}"
            echo "${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" | docker login ghcr.io -u "${{ github.actor }}" --password-stdin
            docker pull "$IMAGE"
            docker stop app || true
            docker rm app || true
            docker run -d --restart=always --name app -p 80:8080 "$IMAGE"
